summary: Frontend-only testing of Talkeez static site. All 14 pages return 200 and render correctly. AAC vocabulary grid, sentence builder, clear/backspace/speak/fullscreen controls work. Mobile nav toggle, FAQ accordion, contact form static success, Stripe donate link + buy button element all verified. One minor observation: /aac.html does NOT include the shared site-nav / site-footer (no data-mount="nav" or "footer"), which contradicts the spec line 'Site nav present on every page'.

ui_bugs.issue: Missing shared site-nav and site-footer (no data-mount="nav"/"footer" tags in page). Every other page renders both via layout.js. Users on AAC tool cannot navigate back to other pages or see footer links. Spec explicitly requires nav on every page.

integration_issues.issue: Console shows 'Failed to fetch' from merchant-ui-api.stripe.com when loading the <stripe-buy-button>. Element renders but inner button cannot reach Stripe from the preview iframe environment (likely sandbox/CORS in preview). Element + key attributes exist correctly; should work in production domain.

action_items:
Add
and
(or include layout.js + the mounts) to /app/frontend/public/aac.html so nav + footer render consistently with the rest of the site as required by spec.
critical_code_review_comments: aac.html missing shared nav/footer mounts — inconsistent with all other pages.
Stripe live publishable key (pk_live_...) is embedded directly in donate.html — acceptable for Buy Buttons but flag for owner awareness.
Console error from Stripe buy-button in preview is expected (third-party iframe); not a code defect.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (13/14 pages fully compliant; aac.html missing nav/footer mounts)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All routes serve 200 with .html extension. AAC interactive flow (categories=8, tile→sentence-strip, clear, category switch) verified via data-testid selectors. Contact form static success works (class becomes 'form-success show'). FAQ accordion toggles 'open' on parent. Donate link href = https://buy.stripe.com/eVqaEXdQTalw5gJ5t48bS00. Mobile nav-links gets 'open' class on toggle. Only outstanding item: add nav/footer to aac.html.
rca_of_the_issue:
aac.html was authored as a standalone immersive page and the developer forgot to include the
and
placeholders that layout.js replaces. Reproduction: open https://aac-communication-1.preview.emergentagent.com/aac.html → no header nav or footer rendered. Mitigation: add both mount divs (and ensure /js/layout.js script tag is included) to aac.html.
